text:
Learning something challenging is one of the hardest things you can do, and the fact that you're sticking with it says everything about your character. Frustration is actually a sign that you care about getting it right and that you're pushing yourself to grow, which is exactly where real progress happens.

The truth is, everyone who's good at anything you admire went through this exact same feeling. They sat where you're sitting now, feeling stuck and questioning whether they could actually do it. The difference between people who succeed and people who give up isn't talent—it's that they kept going through these exact moments.

What you're experiencing right now is your brain rewiring itself. It genuinely feels uncomfortable because it is uncomfortable, but that discomfort is the actual mechanism of learning. In a week or a month, something that feels impossible today will feel obvious, and you won't even remember why it was so hard.

Be patient with yourself. Progress in learning isn't a straight line—it's full of plateaus and breakthroughs. You'll have days where things click and days where you feel like you're going backward. Both are normal. Both are part of it.

You're doing better than you think you are. The fact that you're frustrated means you understand enough to know what you don't understand yet, which is actually a sign of real learning happening. Keep going. You've got this.
